addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Alvaro\generated\regmi_full_submission.csv

1	"""Build a Kaggle-format submission by running the MI ranker over the REAL data.
2	
3	Mirrors the six prediction sets of laurence/dinov2_baseline_modal.py
4	({dataset1,2,3} x {val,test}), but ranks with one of our training-free rankers so
5	we can submit and read the real per-dataset numbers. Kaggle scores omitted queries
6	as 0 and averages ds1/ds2/ds3 x3, so writing a single-dataset (or single-split)
7	file (e.g. --datasets 2 --splits val) lets us read that pool's real MRR in
8	isolation.
9	
10	Rankers:
11	  plain_mi       -- mi_ranker.MIRanker (fast; grid 64).
12	  reg_mi         -- reg_mi_ranker.RegMIRanker (ANTs register-then-MI; grid 96).
13	                    --transform {Affine,SyN} picks the ANTs transform.
14	  affine_argmin  -- affine_ranker.AffineArgminRanker: header-only, ranks by
15	                    ascending affine Frobenius distance. The confirmed ds3
16	                    geometry leak (perfect bijection); runs in seconds.
17	
18	--ranker sets ONE ranker for every dataset. --ranker-map overrides it PER dataset,
19	e.g. "1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:plain_mi" -- the combined submission: ds1/ds3 use plain
20	MI (already ~0.98, registration only costs a tiny -0.019), only ds2 pays for
21	registration. Registration is O(N_queries * N_targets) per pool, so the script
22	prints an upfront cost estimate and projected wall-time BEFORE doing any work.
23	
24	Sharding (registration is embarrassingly parallel over queries): --query-shard i/N
25	processes the i-th of N round-robin slices of the global query list and writes a
26	PARTIAL CSV (<out>.shardI-of-N.csv). --merge-shards N concatenates the N partials
27	into the final --out and checks full coverage. A single un-sharded run (the default,
28	N=1) writes --out directly. NOTE: ANTs already multithreads each registration across
29	all local cores, so local shards give resumability, not speedup; real fan-out needs
30	multiple machines (Modal).
31	
32	Pool isolation is the key invariant: every query is ranked ONLY against its own
33	dataset's own split gallery; we never mix pools. Each ranking is the full
34	space-separated gallery, best->worst.
35	
36	No network, no Kaggle API -- this only writes a CSV locally.
37	
38	    # combined: plain MI for ds1, reg-MI affine for ds2, header leak for ds3 (377 rows)
39	    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . \
40	        --ranker-map "1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:affine_argmin" --transform Affine \
41	        --out Alvaro/generated/regmi_full_submission.csv
42	    # sharded ds2-heavy run, then merge
43	    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission ... --query-shard 1/4 --out OUT.csv
44	    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission ... --merge-shards 4 --out OUT.csv
45	"""
46	
47	from __future__ import annotations
48	
49	import argparse
50	import time
51	from pathlib import Path
52	from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
53	
54	import pandas as pd
55	
56	from .affine_ranker import AffineArgminRanker
57	from .mi_ranker import MIRanker
58	from .reg_mi_ranker import RegMIRanker, REG_GRID
59	
60	RANKER_NAMES = ("plain_mi", "reg_mi", "affine_argmin")
61	
62	ALL_SPLITS = ("val", "test")
63	SUBMISSION_COLS = ["query_id", "target_id_ranking"]
64	# Measured single-threaded ANTs cost per registration at 96^3, per transform.
65	# Affine and SyN are timed; anything else falls back to the affine figure.
66	PER_REG_SEC_BY_TRANSFORM = {"Affine": 0.49, "SyN": 1.24}
67	DEFAULT_PER_REG_SEC = 0.49
68	
69	
70	def resolve(data_root: Path, rel: str) -> Path:
71	    """Absolute image path with the .nii.gz -> .nii fallback (copied from Laurence).
72	
73	    Our local disk holds uncompressed .nii while the CSVs name .nii.gz, so when the
74	    .gz path is absent we retry the bare .nii sibling.
75	    """
76	    p = Path(rel)
77	    p = p if p.is_absolute() else data_root / p
78	    if not p.exists() and p.suffix == ".gz":
79	        nii = p.with_suffix("")  # strip .gz -> try the .nii sibling
80	        if nii.exists():
81	            return nii
82	    return p
83	
84	
85	def _load_set(
86	    data_root: Path, ds: int, split: str
87	) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]] | None:
88	    """Load one (dataset, split) prediction set as ({qid: path}, {tid: path}).
89	
90	    Returns None (with a printed warning) if either CSV is missing. Queries and
91	    targets whose image file is absent on disk are dropped with a warning, so a
92	    missing volume never aborts the whole run.
93	    """
94	    ds_dir = data_root / f"dataset{ds}"
95	    qcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_queries.csv"
96	    gcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_gallery.csv"
97	    if not qcsv.exists() or not gcsv.exists():
98	        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing CSV ({qcsv.name}/{gcsv.name}); skipping set")
99	        return None
100	
101	    def collect(csv_path: Path, id_col: str, img_col: str) -> Dict[str, Path]:
102	        present: Dict[str, Path] = {}
103	        for _, row in pd.read_csv(csv_path).iterrows():
104	            iid = str(row[id_col])
105	            p = resolve(data_root, str(row[img_col]))
106	            if p.exists():
107	                present[iid] = p
108	            else:
109	                print(f"    WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing image for {iid}: {p}")
110	        return present
111	
112	    queries = collect(qcsv, "query_id", "query_image")
113	    targets = collect(gcsv, "target_id", "target_image")
114	    if not queries or not targets:
115	        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: no usable queries/targets on disk; skipping set")
116	        return None
117	    print(f"  dataset{ds}/{split}: {len(queries)} queries, {len(targets)} targets")
118	    return queries, targets
119	
120	
121	def _sanity_block(ranker, queries: Dict[str, Path], targets: Dict[str, Path],
122	                  label: str) -> None:
123	    """Eyeball that the ranker discriminates: top-3 + worst score, first 2 queries.
124	
125	    Works for any ranker exposing score_targets (MIRanker / RegMIRanker). For
126	    reg_mi the printed NMI is POST-registration, which is what we want to eyeball
127	    on real data.
128	    """
129	    print(f"\n=== SANITY ({label}) — does the score separate top from tail? ===")
130	    str_targets = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
131	    for qid in list(queries)[:2]:
132	        scored = ranker.score_targets(str(queries[qid]), str_targets)  # [(tid, nmi)] desc
133	        top3 = scored[:3]
134	        worst_tid, worst_score = scored[-1]
135	        spread = top3[0][1] - worst_score
136	        print(f"  query {qid}:")
137	        for rank, (tid, sc) in enumerate(top3, 1):
138	            print(f"    #{rank}: {tid}  NMI={sc:.4f}")
139	        print(f"    worst: {worst_tid}  NMI={worst_score:.4f}   (top-tail spread={spread:.4f})")
140	        if spread < 0.02:  # NMI lives in ~[1,2]; a real signal spreads far more
141	            print("    !! DEGENERATE: top barely above tail — MI is not discriminating here.")
142	
143	
144	def _abs_out(out: str) -> Path:
145	    """Anchor a relative --out at the repo root, not the CWD."""
146	    p = Path(out)
147	    return p if p.is_absolute() else Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / p
148	
149	
150	def _shard_path(out_path: Path, i: int, n: int) -> Path:
151	    """Partial-CSV path for shard i of n (1-indexed), beside the final --out."""
152	    return out_path.with_name(f"{out_path.stem}.shard{i}-of-{n}{out_path.suffix}")
153	
154	
155	def _parse_ranker_map(spec: str | None, default_name: str, ds_ids: List[int]) -> Dict[int, str]:
156	    """Per-dataset ranker name. --ranker-map 'ds:name,...' overrides --ranker default."""
157	    mapping = {ds: default_name for ds in ds_ids}
158	    if spec:
159	        for item in spec.split(","):
160	            if not item.strip():
161	                continue
162	            ds_str, name = item.split(":")
163	            name = name.strip()
164	            if name not in RANKER_NAMES:
165	                raise SystemExit(f"--ranker-map: unknown ranker '{name}'")
166	            mapping[int(ds_str)] = name
167	    return mapping
168	
169	
170	def _merge_shards(out_path: Path, n: int, n_expected: int, expected_ids: set) -> None:
171	    """Concatenate the n shard partials into the final --out, checking coverage."""
172	    frames = []
173	    for i in range(1, n + 1):
174	        sp = _shard_path(out_path, i, n)
175	        if not sp.exists():
176	            raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: missing shard partial {sp}")
177	        frames.append(pd.read_csv(sp))
178	    merged = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
179	    ids = set(merged["query_id"].astype(str))
180	    dups = len(merged) - merged["query_id"].nunique()
181	    if dups:
182	        raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: {dups} duplicate query rows across shards")
183	    if ids != expected_ids:
184	        missing, extra = expected_ids - ids, ids - expected_ids
185	        raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: coverage mismatch "
186	                         f"(missing {len(missing)}, extra {len(extra)})")
187	    assert len(merged) == n_expected, f"merged {len(merged)} rows, expected {n_expected}"
188	    merged[SUBMISSION_COLS].to_csv(out_path, index=False)
189	    print(f"Merged {n} shards -> {len(merged)} rows at {out_path}")
190	
191	
192	def main(argv: List[str] | None = None) -> None:
193	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
194	    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True,
195	                    help="root that the CSV image paths are relative to")
196	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="Alvaro/generated/mi_submission.csv")
197	    ap.add_argument("--datasets", default="1,2,3",
198	                    help="comma-separated dataset ids to include (e.g. '2')")
199	    ap.add_argument("--splits", default="val,test",
200	                    help="comma-separated splits to include (default 'val,test')")
201	    ap.add_argument("--ranker", choices=list(RANKER_NAMES), default="plain_mi",
202	                    help="ranker for ALL datasets unless overridden by --ranker-map")
203	    ap.add_argument("--ranker-map", default=None,
204	                    help="per-dataset override, e.g. '1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:plain_mi'")
205	    ap.add_argument("--transform", default="Affine",
206	                    help="ANTs type_of_transform for reg_mi (Affine, SyN, ...)")
207	    ap.add_argument("--downsample", type=int, default=None,
208	                    help="reg_mi grid override (default 96); plain_mi is always 64")
209	    ap.add_argument("--query-shard", default="1/1",
210	                    help="process the i-th of N round-robin query slices (e.g. 2/4)")
211	    ap.add_argument("--merge-shards", type=int, default=0,
212	                    help="merge N shard partials into --out and exit")
213	    ap.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true",
214	                    help="print the cost estimate and exit before any ranking")
215	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
216	
217	    data_root = Path(args.data_root).resolve()
218	    ds_ids = sorted(int(x) for x in args.datasets.split(",") if x.strip())
219	    splits = [s for s in ALL_SPLITS if s in
220	              {x.strip() for x in args.splits.split(",")}]  # canonical val,test order
221	    ranker_of_ds = _parse_ranker_map(args.ranker_map, args.ranker, ds_ids)
222	    reg_grid = args.downsample if args.downsample is not None else REG_GRID
223	    out_path = _abs_out(args.out)
224	    print(f"data-root={data_root}  datasets={ds_ids}  splits={splits}\n"
225	          f"ranker_map={ranker_of_ds}  transform={args.transform}  "
226	          f"plain_grid=64 reg_grid={reg_grid}")
227	
228	    # 1) Load every requested prediction set up front (deterministic order: dataset
229	    #    ascending, then val before test) so shard slicing is reproducible.
230	    sets: List[Tuple[int, str, Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]]] = []
231	    for ds in ds_ids:
232	        for split in splits:
233	            loaded = _load_set(data_root, ds, split)
234	            if loaded is not None:
235	                sets.append((ds, split, loaded[0], loaded[1]))
236	    if not sets:
237	        raise SystemExit("No usable prediction sets found; nothing to write.")
238	
239	    n_expected = sum(len(q) for _, _, q, _ in sets)  # queries across all sets
240	    expected_ids = {qid for _, _, q, _ in sets for qid in q}
241	
242	    # Merge mode: combine existing shard partials and exit (no ranking).
243	    if args.merge_shards:
244	        _merge_shards(out_path, args.merge_shards, n_expected, expected_ids)
245	        return
246	
247	    # 2) Flatten to a deterministic global query list, tagging each with its pool's
248	    #    ranker. str_targets is built once per set and shared by reference.
249	    flat: List[Tuple[int, str, str, str, Dict[str, str], str]] = []
250	    set_str_targets = []
251	    for ds, split, queries, targets in sets:
252	        st = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
253	        set_str_targets.append(st)
254	        for qid in sorted(queries):  # sort -> reproducible shard membership
255	            flat.append((ds, split, qid, str(queries[qid]), st, ranker_of_ds[ds]))
256	
257	    # 3) Round-robin shard selection (so the ~140 expensive ds2 queries spread
258	    #    evenly across shards rather than piling into one contiguous chunk).
259	    i, n = (int(x) for x in args.query_shard.split("/"))
260	    if not (1 <= i <= n):
261	        raise SystemExit(f"--query-shard {args.query_shard}: need 1 <= i <= N")
262	    shard = [row for idx, row in enumerate(flat) if idx % n == (i - 1)]
263	
264	    # 4) Upfront cost estimate over THIS shard's queries (reg pairs dominate; plain
265	    #    pairs are negligible). Print BEFORE any heavy work so it can be aborted.
266	    # Per-registration cost depends on the ANTs transform (SyN ~2.5x affine).
267	    per_reg = PER_REG_SEC_BY_TRANSFORM.get(args.transform, DEFAULT_PER_REG_SEC)
268	    reg_pairs = sum(len(st) for _, _, _, _, st, rn in shard if rn == "reg_mi")
269	    est_sec = reg_pairs * per_reg
270	    print(f"\n[cost] shard {i}/{n}: {len(shard)} queries "
271	          f"({sum(rn=='reg_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} reg, "
272	          f"{sum(rn!='reg_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} header/plain)")
273	    print(f"[cost] {reg_pairs} {args.transform} registrations @ ~{per_reg:.2f}s = "
274	          f"~{est_sec/60:.1f} min (~{est_sec/3600:.2f} h) projected wall-time "
275	          f"(+ header/plain MI negligible)")
276	    for ds, split, q, t in sets:
277	        rn = ranker_of_ds[ds]
278	        npairs = len(q) * len(t)
279	        tag = (f"~{npairs*per_reg/60:.1f} min reg" if rn == "reg_mi"
280	               else "negligible (header/plain)")
281	        print(f"       dataset{ds}/{split} [{rn}]: {len(q)}x{len(t)} = {npairs} pairs -> {tag}")
282	
283	    if args.dry_run:
284	        print("\n[dry-run] stopping before any ranking.")
285	        return
286	
287	    # 5) Build only the ranker instances this shard actually needs (RegMIRanker
288	    #    imports ANTs, so skip it if no reg pool is in play). Caches are per-instance
289	    #    and reused across the shard's queries.
290	    need = {rn for *_, rn in shard}
291	    rankers: Dict[str, object] = {}
292	    if "plain_mi" in need:
293	        rankers["plain_mi"] = MIRanker(grid=64)
294	    if "reg_mi" in need:
295	        rankers["reg_mi"] = RegMIRanker(grid=reg_grid, type_of_transform=args.transform)
296	    if "affine_argmin" in need:
297	        rankers["affine_argmin"] = AffineArgminRanker()  # header-only ds3 leak
298	
299	    # 6) Sanity block (only shard 1, to avoid every container repeating it): prefer
300	    #    a reg pool (that's the risky ranker), else dataset1/val, else first set.
301	    if i == 1:
302	        sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d]) for d, s, q, t in sets
303	                       if ranker_of_ds[d] == "reg_mi"), None)
304	        if sanity is None:
305	            d, s, q, t = sets[0]
306	            sanity = (q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d])
307	        _sanity_block(rankers[sanity[3]], sanity[0], sanity[1], sanity[2])
308	
309	    # 7) Rank each query against ITS OWN pool's gallery only (pool isolation).
310	    print("\n=== ranking ===")
311	    t0 = time.time()
312	    rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
313	    # Per-pool top-1 (argmin) target, to check it forms a clean bijection.
314	    pool_top1: Dict[Tuple[int, str], Dict[str, str]] = {}
315	    pool_gallery: Dict[Tuple[int, str], int] = {}
316	    for ds, split, qid, qpath, st_targets, rn in shard:
317	        ranking = rankers[rn].rank(qid, qpath, st_targets)  # full gallery best->worst
318	        # Ranking length == gallery size: every target appears exactly once.
319	        rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
320	        pool_top1.setdefault((ds, split), {})[qid] = ranking[0]
321	        pool_gallery[(ds, split)] = len(st_targets)
322	        if len(rows) % 10 == 0:
323	            print(f"  ranked {len(rows)}/{len(shard)} queries "
324	                  f"({time.time() - t0:.1f}s elapsed)", flush=True)
325	
326	    # Top-1 bijection sanity: a true geometry leak (ds3 affine) makes every query's

"""Build a Kaggle-format submission by running the MI ranker over the REAL data.

Mirrors the six prediction sets of laurence/dinov2_baseline_modal.py
({dataset1,2,3} x {val,test}), but ranks with one of our training-free rankers so
we can submit and read the real per-dataset numbers. Kaggle scores omitted queries
as 0 and averages ds1/ds2/ds3 x3, so writing a single-dataset (or single-split)
file (e.g. --datasets 2 --splits val) lets us read that pool's real MRR in
isolation.

Rankers:
  plain_mi       -- mi_ranker.MIRanker (fast; grid 64).
  reg_mi         -- reg_mi_ranker.RegMIRanker (ANTs register-then-MI; grid 96).
                    --transform {Affine,SyN} picks the ANTs transform.
  affine_argmin  -- affine_ranker.AffineArgminRanker: header-only, ranks by
                    ascending affine Frobenius distance. The confirmed ds3
                    geometry leak (perfect bijection); runs in seconds.

--ranker sets ONE ranker for every dataset. --ranker-map overrides it PER dataset,
e.g. "1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:plain_mi" -- the combined submission: ds1/ds3 use plain
MI (already ~0.98, registration only costs a tiny -0.019), only ds2 pays for
registration. Registration is O(N_queries * N_targets) per pool, so the script
prints an upfront cost estimate and projected wall-time BEFORE doing any work.

Sharding (registration is embarrassingly parallel over queries): --query-shard i/N
processes the i-th of N round-robin slices of the global query list and writes a
PARTIAL CSV (<out>.shardI-of-N.csv). --merge-shards N concatenates the N partials
into the final --out and checks full coverage. A single un-sharded run (the default,
N=1) writes --out directly. NOTE: ANTs already multithreads each registration across
all local cores, so local shards give resumability, not speedup; real fan-out needs
multiple machines (Modal).

Pool isolation is the key invariant: every query is ranked ONLY against its own
dataset's own split gallery; we never mix pools. Each ranking is the full
space-separated gallery, best->worst.

No network, no Kaggle API -- this only writes a CSV locally.

    # combined: plain MI for ds1, reg-MI affine for ds2, header leak for ds3 (377 rows)
    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . \
        --ranker-map "1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:affine_argmin" --transform Affine \
        --out Alvaro/generated/regmi_full_submission.csv
    # sharded ds2-heavy run, then merge
    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission ... --query-shard 1/4 --out OUT.csv
    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission ... --merge-shards 4 --out OUT.csv
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple

import pandas as pd

from .affine_ranker import AffineArgminRanker
from .mi_ranker import MIRanker
from .reg_mi_ranker import RegMIRanker, REG_GRID

RANKER_NAMES = ("plain_mi", "reg_mi", "affine_argmin")

ALL_SPLITS = ("val", "test")
SUBMISSION_COLS = ["query_id", "target_id_ranking"]
# Measured single-threaded ANTs cost per registration at 96^3, per transform.
# Affine and SyN are timed; anything else falls back to the affine figure.
PER_REG_SEC_BY_TRANSFORM = {"Affine": 0.49, "SyN": 1.24}
DEFAULT_PER_REG_SEC = 0.49


def resolve(data_root: Path, rel: str) -> Path:
    """Absolute image path with the .nii.gz -> .nii fallback (copied from Laurence).

    Our local disk holds uncompressed .nii while the CSVs name .nii.gz, so when the
    .gz path is absent we retry the bare .nii sibling.
    """
    p = Path(rel)
    p = p if p.is_absolute() else data_root / p
    if not p.exists() and p.suffix == ".gz":
        nii = p.with_suffix("")  # strip .gz -> try the .nii sibling
        if nii.exists():
            return nii
    return p


def _load_set(
    data_root: Path, ds: int, split: str
) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]] | None:
    """Load one (dataset, split) prediction set as ({qid: path}, {tid: path}).

    Returns None (with a printed warning) if either CSV is missing. Queries and
    targets whose image file is absent on disk are dropped with a warning, so a
    missing volume never aborts the whole run.
    """
    ds_dir = data_root / f"dataset{ds}"
    qcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_queries.csv"
    gcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_gallery.csv"
    if not qcsv.exists() or not gcsv.exists():
        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing CSV ({qcsv.name}/{gcsv.name}); skipping set")
        return None

    def collect(csv_path: Path, id_col: str, img_col: str) -> Dict[str, Path]:
        present: Dict[str, Path] = {}
        for _, row in pd.read_csv(csv_path).iterrows():
            iid = str(row[id_col])
            p = resolve(data_root, str(row[img_col]))
            if p.exists():
                present[iid] = p
            else:
                print(f"    WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing image for {iid}: {p}")
        return present

    queries = collect(qcsv, "query_id", "query_image")
    targets = collect(gcsv, "target_id", "target_image")
    if not queries or not targets:
        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: no usable queries/targets on disk; skipping set")
        return None
    print(f"  dataset{ds}/{split}: {len(queries)} queries, {len(targets)} targets")
    return queries, targets


def _sanity_block(ranker, queries: Dict[str, Path], targets: Dict[str, Path],
                  label: str) -> None:
    """Eyeball that the ranker discriminates: top-3 + worst score, first 2 queries.

    Works for any ranker exposing score_targets (MIRanker / RegMIRanker). For
    reg_mi the printed NMI is POST-registration, which is what we want to eyeball
    on real data.
    """
    print(f"\n=== SANITY ({label}) — does the score separate top from tail? ===")
    str_targets = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
    for qid in list(queries)[:2]:
        scored = ranker.score_targets(str(queries[qid]), str_targets)  # [(tid, nmi)] desc
        top3 = scored[:3]
        worst_tid, worst_score = scored[-1]
        spread = top3[0][1] - worst_score
        print(f"  query {qid}:")
        for rank, (tid, sc) in enumerate(top3, 1):
            print(f"    #{rank}: {tid}  NMI={sc:.4f}")
        print(f"    worst: {worst_tid}  NMI={worst_score:.4f}   (top-tail spread={spread:.4f})")
        if spread < 0.02:  # NMI lives in ~[1,2]; a real signal spreads far more
            print("    !! DEGENERATE: top barely above tail — MI is not discriminating here.")


def _abs_out(out: str) -> Path:
    """Anchor a relative --out at the repo root, not the CWD."""
    p = Path(out)
    return p if p.is_absolute() else Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / p


def _shard_path(out_path: Path, i: int, n: int) -> Path:
    """Partial-CSV path for shard i of n (1-indexed), beside the final --out."""
    return out_path.with_name(f"{out_path.stem}.shard{i}-of-{n}{out_path.suffix}")


def _parse_ranker_map(spec: str | None, default_name: str, ds_ids: List[int]) -> Dict[int, str]:
    """Per-dataset ranker name. --ranker-map 'ds:name,...' overrides --ranker default."""
    mapping = {ds: default_name for ds in ds_ids}
    if spec:
        for item in spec.split(","):
            if not item.strip():
                continue
            ds_str, name = item.split(":")
            name = name.strip()
            if name not in RANKER_NAMES:
                raise SystemExit(f"--ranker-map: unknown ranker '{name}'")
            mapping[int(ds_str)] = name
    return mapping


def _merge_shards(out_path: Path, n: int, n_expected: int, expected_ids: set) -> None:
    """Concatenate the n shard partials into the final --out, checking coverage."""
    frames = []
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        sp = _shard_path(out_path, i, n)
        if not sp.exists():
            raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: missing shard partial {sp}")
        frames.append(pd.read_csv(sp))
    merged = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
    ids = set(merged["query_id"].astype(str))
    dups = len(merged) - merged["query_id"].nunique()
    if dups:
        raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: {dups} duplicate query rows across shards")
    if ids != expected_ids:
        missing, extra = expected_ids - ids, ids - expected_ids
        raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: coverage mismatch "
                         f"(missing {len(missing)}, extra {len(extra)})")
    assert len(merged) == n_expected, f"merged {len(merged)} rows, expected {n_expected}"
    merged[SUBMISSION_COLS].to_csv(out_path, index=False)
    print(f"Merged {n} shards -> {len(merged)} rows at {out_path}")


def main(argv: List[str] | None = None) -> None:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True,
                    help="root that the CSV image paths are relative to")
    ap.add_argument("--out", default="Alvaro/generated/mi_submission.csv")
    ap.add_argument("--datasets", default="1,2,3",
                    help="comma-separated dataset ids to include (e.g. '2')")
    ap.add_argument("--splits", default="val,test",
                    help="comma-separated splits to include (default 'val,test')")
    ap.add_argument("--ranker", choices=list(RANKER_NAMES), default="plain_mi",
                    help="ranker for ALL datasets unless overridden by --ranker-map")
    ap.add_argument("--ranker-map", default=None,
                    help="per-dataset override, e.g. '1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:plain_mi'")
    ap.add_argument("--transform", default="Affine",
                    help="ANTs type_of_transform for reg_mi (Affine, SyN, ...)")
    ap.add_argument("--downsample", type=int, default=None,
                    help="reg_mi grid override (default 96); plain_mi is always 64")
    ap.add_argument("--query-shard", default="1/1",
                    help="process the i-th of N round-robin query slices (e.g. 2/4)")
    ap.add_argument("--merge-shards", type=int, default=0,
                    help="merge N shard partials into --out and exit")
    ap.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true",
                    help="print the cost estimate and exit before any ranking")
    args = ap.parse_args(argv)

    data_root = Path(args.data_root).resolve()
    ds_ids = sorted(int(x) for x in args.datasets.split(",") if x.strip())
    splits = [s for s in ALL_SPLITS if s in
              {x.strip() for x in args.splits.split(",")}]  # canonical val,test order
    ranker_of_ds = _parse_ranker_map(args.ranker_map, args.ranker, ds_ids)
    reg_grid = args.downsample if args.downsample is not None else REG_GRID
    out_path = _abs_out(args.out)
    print(f"data-root={data_root}  datasets={ds_ids}  splits={splits}\n"
          f"ranker_map={ranker_of_ds}  transform={args.transform}  "
          f"plain_grid=64 reg_grid={reg_grid}")

    # 1) Load every requested prediction set up front (deterministic order: dataset
    #    ascending, then val before test) so shard slicing is reproducible.
    sets: List[Tuple[int, str, Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]]] = []
    for ds in ds_ids:
        for split in splits:
            loaded = _load_set(data_root, ds, split)
            if loaded is not None:
                sets.append((ds, split, loaded[0], loaded[1]))
    if not sets:
        raise SystemExit("No usable prediction sets found; nothing to write.")

    n_expected = sum(len(q) for _, _, q, _ in sets)  # queries across all sets
    expected_ids = {qid for _, _, q, _ in sets for qid in q}

    # Merge mode: combine existing shard partials and exit (no ranking).
    if args.merge_shards:
        _merge_shards(out_path, args.merge_shards, n_expected, expected_ids)
        return

    # 2) Flatten to a deterministic global query list, tagging each with its pool's
    #    ranker. str_targets is built once per set and shared by reference.
    flat: List[Tuple[int, str, str, str, Dict[str, str], str]] = []
    set_str_targets = []
    for ds, split, queries, targets in sets:
        st = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
        set_str_targets.append(st)
        for qid in sorted(queries):  # sort -> reproducible shard membership
            flat.append((ds, split, qid, str(queries[qid]), st, ranker_of_ds[ds]))

    # 3) Round-robin shard selection (so the ~140 expensive ds2 queries spread
    #    evenly across shards rather than piling into one contiguous chunk).
    i, n = (int(x) for x in args.query_shard.split("/"))
    if not (1 <= i <= n):
        raise SystemExit(f"--query-shard {args.query_shard}: need 1 <= i <= N")
    shard = [row for idx, row in enumerate(flat) if idx % n == (i - 1)]

    # 4) Upfront cost estimate over THIS shard's queries (reg pairs dominate; plain
    #    pairs are negligible). Print BEFORE any heavy work so it can be aborted.
    # Per-registration cost depends on the ANTs transform (SyN ~2.5x affine).
    per_reg = PER_REG_SEC_BY_TRANSFORM.get(args.transform, DEFAULT_PER_REG_SEC)
    reg_pairs = sum(len(st) for _, _, _, _, st, rn in shard if rn == "reg_mi")
    est_sec = reg_pairs * per_reg
    print(f"\n[cost] shard {i}/{n}: {len(shard)} queries "
          f"({sum(rn=='reg_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} reg, "
          f"{sum(rn!='reg_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} header/plain)")
    print(f"[cost] {reg_pairs} {args.transform} registrations @ ~{per_reg:.2f}s = "
          f"~{est_sec/60:.1f} min (~{est_sec/3600:.2f} h) projected wall-time "
          f"(+ header/plain MI negligible)")
    for ds, split, q, t in sets:
        rn = ranker_of_ds[ds]
        npairs = len(q) * len(t)
        tag = (f"~{npairs*per_reg/60:.1f} min reg" if rn == "reg_mi"
               else "negligible (header/plain)")
        print(f"       dataset{ds}/{split} [{rn}]: {len(q)}x{len(t)} = {npairs} pairs -> {tag}")

    if args.dry_run:
        print("\n[dry-run] stopping before any ranking.")
        return

    # 5) Build only the ranker instances this shard actually needs (RegMIRanker
    #    imports ANTs, so skip it if no reg pool is in play). Caches are per-instance
    #    and reused across the shard's queries.
    need = {rn for *_, rn in shard}
    rankers: Dict[str, object] = {}
    if "plain_mi" in need:
        rankers["plain_mi"] = MIRanker(grid=64)
    if "reg_mi" in need:
        rankers["reg_mi"] = RegMIRanker(grid=reg_grid, type_of_transform=args.transform)
    if "affine_argmin" in need:
        rankers["affine_argmin"] = AffineArgminRanker()  # header-only ds3 leak

    # 6) Sanity block (only shard 1, to avoid every container repeating it): prefer
    #    a reg pool (that's the risky ranker), else dataset1/val, else first set.
    if i == 1:
        sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d]) for d, s, q, t in sets
                       if ranker_of_ds[d] == "reg_mi"), None)
        if sanity is None:
            d, s, q, t = sets[0]
            sanity = (q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d])
        _sanity_block(rankers[sanity[3]], sanity[0], sanity[1], sanity[2])

    # 7) Rank each query against ITS OWN pool's gallery only (pool isolation).
    print("\n=== ranking ===")
    t0 = time.time()
    rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
    # Per-pool top-1 (argmin) target, to check it forms a clean bijection.
    pool_top1: Dict[Tuple[int, str], Dict[str, str]] = {}
    pool_gallery: Dict[Tuple[int, str], int] = {}
    for ds, split, qid, qpath, st_targets, rn in shard:
        ranking = rankers[rn].rank(qid, qpath, st_targets)  # full gallery best->worst
        # Ranking length == gallery size: every target appears exactly once.
        rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
        pool_top1.setdefault((ds, split), {})[qid] = ranking[0]
        pool_gallery[(ds, split)] = len(st_targets)
        if len(rows) % 10 == 0:
            print(f"  ranked {len(rows)}/{len(shard)} queries "
                  f"({time.time() - t0:.1f}s elapsed)", flush=True)

    # Top-1 bijection sanity: a true geometry leak (ds3 affine) makes every query's
    # argmin a DISTINCT target, so the chosen top-1s are a permutation of the
    # gallery. NOTE: only meaningful for a full (un-sharded) pool; a round-robin
    # shard sees a query subset, so collisions there are expected, not a bug.
    print("\n=== TOP-1 BIJECTION SANITY (each pool: are argmin targets distinct?) ===")
    for (ds, split), top1 in pool_top1.items():
        chosen = list(top1.values())
        distinct, nq, ng = len(set(chosen)), len(chosen), pool_gallery[(ds, split)]
        bij = distinct == nq == ng
        print(f"  dataset{ds}/{split}: {distinct}/{nq} distinct top-1 targets "
              f"(gallery={ng}) -> {'clean bijection' if bij else 'NOT a bijection'}")
        if not bij and n == 1:  # only warn on a complete pool, not a partial shard
            from collections import Counter
            dups = [t for t, c in Counter(chosen).items() if c > 1]
            print(f"    !! WARN: {len(dups)} target(s) picked by >1 query "
                  f"(leak NOT clean): {dups[:8]}{' ...' if len(dups) > 8 else ''}")

    # 8) Write + verify. Single run (N=1) writes --out and must total n_expected;
    #    a shard writes a partial and must total this shard's query count.
    assert len(rows) == len(shard), f"wrote {len(rows)} rows, expected {len(shard)}"
    dest = out_path if n == 1 else _shard_path(out_path, i, n)
    dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=SUBMISSION_COLS).to_csv(dest, index=False)
    print(f"\nWrote {len(rows)} rows to {dest}  (total {time.time() - t0:.1f}s)")
    if n == 1:
        assert len(rows) == n_expected, f"total {len(rows)} != expected {n_expected}"
    else:
        print(f"Shard {i}/{n} done. Merge with: --merge-shards {n} --out {args.out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

1	"""Lightweight NMI cross-modal MRI retrieval on Modal.
2	
3	Algorithm (pure numpy, no registration library):
4	  1. Load every unique volume once with ThreadPoolExecutor.
5	  2. Downsample to ~32 voxels per dimension (stride).
6	  3. Translate each volume so its foreground centre-of-mass sits at the image
7	     centre.  This corrects the dominant translation component of ds2's rigid

"""Lightweight NMI cross-modal MRI retrieval on Modal.

Algorithm (pure numpy, no registration library):
  1. Load every unique volume once with ThreadPoolExecutor.
  2. Downsample to ~32 voxels per dimension (stride).
  3. Translate each volume so its foreground centre-of-mass sits at the image
     centre.  This corrects the dominant translation component of ds2's rigid
     deformations without any iterative optimisation.
  4. Compute NMI pairwise from 2-D joint histograms (32 bins).
     - ds1/ds2: restrict to the non-zero union mask of each pair.
     - ds3:     restrict to the non-zero intersection mask so the surgical
                cavity (signal=0) doesn't drag NMI down.

Dependencies: nibabel, numpy only.  No SimpleITK.
Expected runtime on cpu=8: ~45-60 s total.

Run:
    modal run laurence/mattes_mi_modal.py
    modal run laurence/mattes_mi_modal.py --out my_submission.csv
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import csv
import io
from pathlib import Path

import modal

app = modal.App("ehl-mattes-mi")
vol = modal.Volume.from_name("ehl-2026-vol-2")

image = (
    modal.Image.debian_slim(python_version="3.12")
    .pip_install(
        "nibabel>=5.3",
        "numpy>=2.0",
    )
)

TARGET_DIM = 32   # downsample each axis to at most this many voxels
NMI_BINS   = 32   # histogram bins per axis


@app.function(
    image=image,
    volumes={"/data": vol},
    timeout=600,
    memory=8192,
    cpu=8,
)
def run_mattes_mi() -> str:
    import csv as _csv
    import io as _io
    from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
    from pathlib import Path as _Path

    import nibabel as nib
    import numpy as np

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- helpers

    def find_data_root(mount: _Path) -> _Path:
        for p in sorted(mount.rglob("dataset1")):
            if p.is_dir():
                found = p.parent
                print(f"Data root: {found}")
                return found
        raise RuntimeError(f"No dataset1/ under {mount}")

    def read_csv(path: _Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
        with path.open(newline="") as f:
            return list(_csv.DictReader(f))

    def resolve(rel: str, root: _Path) -> _Path:
        p = _Path(rel)
        if not p.is_absolute():
            p = root / p
        if not p.exists() and p.suffix == ".gz":
            alt = p.with_suffix("")
            if alt.exists():
                return alt
        return p

    data_root = find_data_root(_Path("/data"))

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- manifests

    SPECS = [
        ("dataset1", "val"),
        ("dataset1", "test"),
        ("dataset2", "val"),
        ("dataset2", "test"),
        ("dataset3", "val"),
        ("dataset3", "test"),
    ]

    prediction_sets: list[dict] = []
    for ds, split in SPECS:
        qcsv = data_root / ds / f"{split}_queries.csv"
        gcsv = data_root / ds / f"{split}_gallery.csv"
        if not qcsv.exists() or not gcsv.exists():
            print(f"Skipping {ds}/{split}: CSV missing")
            continue
        queries = {r["query_id"]: resolve(r["query_image"], data_root) for r in read_csv(qcsv)}
        targets = {r["target_id"]: resolve(r["target_image"], data_root) for r in read_csv(gcsv)}
        queries = {k: v for k, v in queries.items() if v.exists()}
        targets = {k: v for k, v in targets.items() if v.exists()}
        if queries and targets:
            prediction_sets.append({"ds": ds, "split": split, "queries": queries, "targets": targets})
            print(f"  {ds}/{split}: {len(queries)} queries, {len(targets)} targets")

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------
    # Load, downsample, and centre-of-mass align all unique volumes
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------

    all_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
    for ps in prediction_sets:
        all_paths.update(ps["queries"])
        all_paths.update(ps["targets"])

    print(f"\nLoading {len(all_paths)} unique volumes...")

    def _load(item: tuple) -> tuple[str, np.ndarray]:
        img_id, path = item
        arr = nib.load(str(path)).get_fdata(dtype=np.float32)
        if arr.ndim == 4:
            arr = arr[..., 0]
        arr = np.nan_to_num(arr, nan=0.0, posinf=0.0, neginf=0.0)

        # Stride downsample then crop/pad to exactly (TARGET_DIM,) per axis.
        # Volumes across datasets have different shapes, so we normalise here
        # to guarantee all cached arrays have the same flat length for NMI.
        steps = tuple(max(1, s // TARGET_DIM) for s in arr.shape)
        arr = arr[::steps[0], ::steps[1], ::steps[2]]
        out = np.zeros((TARGET_DIM, TARGET_DIM, TARGET_DIM), dtype=np.float32)
        s = tuple(min(arr.shape[i], TARGET_DIM) for i in range(3))
        out[:s[0], :s[1], :s[2]] = arr[:s[0], :s[1], :s[2]]
        arr = out

        # Translate so the foreground centre-of-mass sits at the image centre.
        # Corrects the dominant translation component of ds2 rigid deformations.
        fg = arr > 0
        if fg.any():
            cm     = np.argwhere(fg).mean(axis=0)
            centre = np.array(arr.shape) / 2.0
            shift  = np.round(centre - cm).astype(int)
            arr = np.roll(arr, (shift[0], shift[1], shift[2]), axis=(0, 1, 2))

        return img_id, arr

    vol_cache: dict[str, np.ndarray] = {}
    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=16) as ex:
        for img_id, arr in ex.map(_load, sorted(all_paths.items())):
            vol_cache[img_id] = arr

    example = next(iter(vol_cache.values()))
    print(f"Loaded {len(vol_cache)} volumes. Downsampled shape: {example.shape}")

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------
    # NMI scoring
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------

    def nmi(a: np.ndarray, b: np.ndarray, intersect_mask: bool = False) -> float:
        """Normalised mutual information from a 2-D joint histogram."""
        a_f = a.ravel(); b_f = b.ravel()
        if intersect_mask:
            mask = (a_f != 0) & (b_f != 0)   # ds3: ignore zero-signal cavity
        else:
            mask = (a_f != 0) | (b_f != 0)   # ds1/ds2: use any foreground
        if mask.sum() < 50:
            return 0.0
        h, _, _ = np.histogram2d(a_f[mask], b_f[mask], bins=NMI_BINS)
        h /= h.sum() + 1e-10
        pa  = h.sum(axis=1); pb = h.sum(axis=0)
        ha  = -(pa  * np.log(pa  + 1e-10)).sum()
        hb  = -(pb  * np.log(pb  + 1e-10)).sum()
        hab = -(h   * np.log(h   + 1e-10)).sum()
        v   = float((ha + hb) / (hab + 1e-10))
        return v if np.isfinite(v) else 0.0

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- rank and write

    print("\n=== Scoring all retrieval pools ===")

    rows: list[dict[str, str]] = []
    for ps in prediction_sets:
        ds    = ps["ds"]
        split = ps["split"]
        use_intersect = ds == "dataset3"

        q_ids = sorted(k for k in ps["queries"] if k in vol_cache)
        t_ids = sorted(k for k in ps["targets"] if k in vol_cache)
        nq, nt = len(q_ids), len(t_ids)
        print(f"{ds}/{split}: {nq}×{nt} pairs")

        scores = np.zeros((nq, nt), dtype=np.float32)
        for i, qid in enumerate(q_ids):
            if i % 20 == 0:
                print(f"  row {i}/{nq}")
            q_arr = vol_cache[qid]
            for j, tid in enumerate(t_ids):
                scores[i, j] = nmi(q_arr, vol_cache[tid], intersect_mask=use_intersect)

        for i, qid in enumerate(q_ids):
            ranked = [t_ids[j] for j in np.argsort(-scores[i])]
            rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranked)})

    buf = _io.StringIO()
    writer = _csv.DictWriter(buf, fieldnames=["query_id", "target_id_ranking"])
    writer.writeheader()
    writer.writerows(rows)
    print(f"\nGenerated {len(rows)} submission rows.")
    return buf.getvalue()


@app.local_entrypoint()
def main(out: str = "mattes_mi_submission.csv") -> None:
    print("Running lightweight NMI retrieval on Modal...")
    csv_content = run_mattes_mi.remote()
    out_path = Path(out)
    out_path.write_text(csv_content, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"Saved {len(csv_content.splitlines()) - 1} rows to {out_path}")

"""Register-then-MI ranking function (ANTs affine + reused NMI).

Plain MI (mi_ranker.py) assumes the query and target already share a grid, so it
collapses on ds2 where the two volumes were deformed INDEPENDENTLY. Here we first
register each target to the query, then score MI on the REGISTERED pair. If the
ds2 collapse is purely a misalignment problem, registration should recover it.

Per query<->target pair:
  1. load + downsample both volumes to grid^3 (default 96; registration needs more
     detail than the 64 plain MI uses),
  2. ants.registration(fixed=query, moving=target, type_of_transform=...) -> take
     the "warpedmovout" (the target resampled into the query's space),
  3. NMI(query, registered_target) via the EXISTING mi_ranker code (digitize_array
     + _nmi -- not reimplemented),
  4. rank gallery targets by that post-registration NMI.

type_of_transform is a parameter (default "Affine") so we can later try "SyN".

Same RankFn interface as evaluate.py expects:
    rank(query_id, query_path, {target_id: target_path}) -> [target_id ...]

1	"""Mutual-information ranking function (training-free), CPU-only.
2	
3	Score each query<->target pair by the NORMALIZED MUTUAL INFORMATION of their
4	voxel intensities on a shared downsampled grid:
5	
6	    NMI = (H(A) + H(B)) / H(A, B)
7	
8	which is ~1 when the two volumes are statistically independent and ~2 when one
9	perfectly predicts the other; higher = more similar. MI is the classic
10	cross-modal (T1<->T2) registration similarity precisely because it does not
11	assume matching intensities, only matching STRUCTURE.
12	
13	The crux is the SHARED GRID: every volume is resampled to a fixed 64^3 index
14	grid, so corresponding indices mean corresponding anatomy ONLY when the volumes
15	are spatially aligned. That is true for ds1 (registered) and roughly true for ds3
16	(~same space), but broken for ds2 (independent deformation) -- which is exactly
17	the failure pattern we want to expose.
18	
19	Run across the three pools:
20	    python -m Alvaro.src.mi_ranker

"""Mutual-information ranking function (training-free), CPU-only.

Score each query<->target pair by the NORMALIZED MUTUAL INFORMATION of their
voxel intensities on a shared downsampled grid:

    NMI = (H(A) + H(B)) / H(A, B)

which is ~1 when the two volumes are statistically independent and ~2 when one
perfectly predicts the other; higher = more similar. MI is the classic
cross-modal (T1<->T2) registration similarity precisely because it does not
assume matching intensities, only matching STRUCTURE.

The crux is the SHARED GRID: every volume is resampled to a fixed 64^3 index
grid, so corresponding indices mean corresponding anatomy ONLY when the volumes
are spatially aligned. That is true for ds1 (registered) and roughly true for ds3
(~same space), but broken for ds2 (independent deformation) -- which is exactly
the failure pattern we want to expose.

Run across the three pools:
    python -m Alvaro.src.mi_ranker

"""Build a Kaggle-format submission by running the MI ranker over the REAL data.

Mirrors the six prediction sets of laurence/dinov2_baseline_modal.py
({dataset1,2,3} x {val,test}), but ranks with one of our training-free rankers so
we can submit and read the real per-dataset numbers. Kaggle scores omitted queries
as 0 and averages ds1/ds2/ds3 x3, so writing a single-dataset (or single-split)
file (e.g. --datasets 2 --splits val) lets us read that pool's real MRR in
isolation.

Rankers:
  plain_mi       -- mi_ranker.MIRanker (fast; grid 64).
  reg_mi         -- reg_mi_ranker.RegMIRanker (ANTs register-then-MI; grid 96).
                    --transform {Affine,SyN} picks the ANTs transform.
  affine_argmin  -- affine_ranker.AffineArgminRanker: header-only, ranks by
                    ascending affine Frobenius distance. The confirmed ds3
                    geometry leak (perfect bijection); runs in seconds.

--ranker sets ONE ranker for every dataset. --ranker-map overrides it PER dataset,
e.g. "1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:plain_mi" -- the combined submission: ds1/ds3 use plain
MI (already ~0.98, registration only costs a tiny -0.019), only ds2 pays for
registration. Registration is O(N_queries * N_targets) per pool, so the script
prints an upfront cost estimate and projected wall-time BEFORE doing any work.

Sharding (registration is embarrassingly parallel over queries): --query-shard i/N
processes the i-th of N round-robin slices of the global query list and writes a
PARTIAL CSV (<out>.shardI-of-N.csv). --merge-shards N concatenates the N partials
into the final --out and checks full coverage. A single un-sharded run (the default,
N=1) writes --out directly. NOTE: ANTs already multithreads each registration across
all local cores, so local shards give resumability, not speedup; real fan-out needs
multiple machines (Modal).

Pool isolation is the key invariant: every query is ranked ONLY against its own
dataset's own split gallery; we never mix pools. Each ranking is the full
space-separated gallery, best->worst.

No network, no Kaggle API -- this only writes a CSV locally.

Run from the repo root (one level above this file):

    # combined: plain MI for ds1, reg-MI affine for ds2, header leak for ds3 (377 rows)
    python laurence/make_submission.py --data-root . \\

from Alvaro.src.mi_ranker import MIRanker  # noqa: E402
from Alvaro.src.reg_mi_ranker import RegMIRanker, REG_GRID  # noqa: E402

RANKER_NAMES = ("plain_mi", "reg_mi", "affine_argmin")

ALL_SPLITS = ("val", "test")
SUBMISSION_COLS = ["query_id", "target_id_ranking"]
PER_REG_SEC_BY_TRANSFORM = {"Affine": 0.49, "SyN": 1.24}
DEFAULT_PER_REG_SEC = 0.49


def resolve(data_root: Path, rel: str) -> Path:

def _load_set(
    data_root: Path, ds: int, split: str
) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]] | None:
    ds_dir = data_root / f"dataset{ds}"
    qcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_queries.csv"
    gcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_gallery.csv"

        present: Dict[str, Path] = {}
        for _, row in pd.read_csv(csv_path).iterrows():
            iid = str(row[id_col])
            p = resolve(data_root, str(row[img_col]))
            if p.exists():
                present[iid] = p
            else:

    queries = collect(qcsv, "query_id", "query_image")
    targets = collect(gcsv, "target_id", "target_image")

    return out_path.with_name(f"{out_path.stem}.shard{i}-of-{n}{out_path.suffix}")


def _parse_ranker_map(spec: str | None, default_name: str, ds_ids: List[int]) -> Dict[int, str]:
    mapping = {ds: default_name for ds in ds_ids}
    if spec:
        for item in spec.split(","):

            raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: missing shard partial {sp}")

    if ids != expected_ids:
        missing, extra = expected_ids - ids, ids - expected_ids
        raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: coverage mismatch "
                         f"(missing {len(missing)}, extra {len(extra)})")

    print(f"Merged {n} shards -> {len(merged)} rows at {out_path}")


def main(argv: List[str] | None = None) -> None:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)

                    help="root that the CSV image paths are relative to")
    ap.add_argument("--out", default="Alvaro/generated/mi_submission.csv")

                    help="comma-separated dataset ids to include (e.g. '2')")

                    help="per-dataset override, e.g. '1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:plain_mi'")
    ap.add_argument("--transform", default="Affine",
                    help="ANTs type_of_transform for reg_mi (Affine, SyN, ...)")
    ap.add_argument("--downsample", type=int, default=None,
                    help="reg_mi grid override (default 96); plain_mi is always 64")

                    help="process the i-th of N round-robin query slices (e.g. 2/4)")

    ds_ids = sorted(int(x) for x in args.datasets.split(",") if x.strip())
    splits = [s for s in ALL_SPLITS if s in
              {x.strip() for x in args.splits.split(",")}]
    ranker_of_ds = _parse_ranker_map(args.ranker_map, args.ranker, ds_ids)
    reg_grid = args.downsample if args.downsample is not None else REG_GRID
    out_path = _abs_out(args.out)
    print(f"data-root={data_root}  datasets={ds_ids}  splits={splits}\n"

          f"plain_grid=64 reg_grid={reg_grid}")

    expected_ids = {qid for _, _, q, _ in sets for qid in q}

    if args.merge_shards:
        _merge_shards(out_path, args.merge_shards, n_expected, expected_ids)
        return

    set_str_targets = []
    for ds, split, queries, targets in sets:
        st = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
        set_str_targets.append(st)
        for qid in sorted(queries):
            flat.append((ds, split, qid, str(queries[qid]), st, ranker_of_ds[ds]))

        raise SystemExit(f"--query-shard {args.query_shard}: need 1 <= i <= N")
    shard = [row for idx, row in enumerate(flat) if idx % n == (i - 1)]

    per_reg = PER_REG_SEC_BY_TRANSFORM.get(args.transform, DEFAULT_PER_REG_SEC)
    reg_pairs = sum(len(st) for _, _, _, _, st, rn in shard if rn == "reg_mi")
    est_sec = reg_pairs * per_reg
    print(f"\n[cost] shard {i}/{n}: {len(shard)} queries "
          f"({sum(rn=='reg_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} reg, "
          f"{sum(rn!='reg_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} header/plain)")
    print(f"[cost] {reg_pairs} {args.transform} registrations @ ~{per_reg:.2f}s = "
          f"~{est_sec/60:.1f} min (~{est_sec/3600:.2f} h) projected wall-time "
          f"(+ header/plain MI negligible)")

        rn = ranker_of_ds[ds]
        npairs = len(q) * len(t)
        tag = (f"~{npairs*per_reg/60:.1f} min reg" if rn == "reg_mi"
               else "negligible (header/plain)")
        print(f"       dataset{ds}/{split} [{rn}]: {len(q)}x{len(t)} = {npairs} pairs -> {tag}")

        print("\n[dry-run] stopping before any ranking.")

    rankers: Dict[str, object] = {}

        rankers["plain_mi"] = MIRanker(grid=64)
    if "reg_mi" in need:
        rankers["reg_mi"] = RegMIRanker(grid=reg_grid, type_of_transform=args.transform)
    if "affine_argmin" in need:
        rankers["affine_argmin"] = AffineArgminRanker()

    if i == 1:
        sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d]) for d, s, q, t in sets
                       if ranker_of_ds[d] == "reg_mi"), None)
        if sanity is None:
            d, s, q, t = sets[0]
            sanity = (q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d])
        _sanity_block(rankers[sanity[3]], sanity[0], sanity[1], sanity[2])

    print("\n=== ranking ===")
    t0 = time.time()
    rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
    pool_top1: Dict[Tuple[int, str], Dict[str, str]] = {}
    pool_gallery: Dict[Tuple[int, str], int] = {}
    for ds, split, qid, qpath, st_targets, rn in shard:
        ranking = rankers[rn].rank(qid, qpath, st_targets)
        rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
        pool_top1.setdefault((ds, split), {})[qid] = ranking[0]
        pool_gallery[(ds, split)] = len(st_targets)
        if len(rows) % 10 == 0:
            print(f"  ranked {len(rows)}/{len(shard)} queries "
                  f"({time.time() - t0:.1f}s elapsed)", flush=True)

    print("\n=== TOP-1 BIJECTION SANITY (each pool: are argmin targets distinct?) ===")
    for (ds, split), top1 in pool_top1.items():
        chosen = list(top1.values())
        distinct, nq, ng = len(set(chosen)), len(chosen), pool_gallery[(ds, split)]
        bij = distinct == nq == ng
        print(f"  dataset{ds}/{split}: {distinct}/{nq} distinct top-1 targets "
              f"(gallery={ng}) -> {'clean bijection' if bij else 'NOT a bijection'}")
        if not bij and n == 1:
            from collections import Counter
            dups = [t for t, c in Counter(chosen).items() if c > 1]
            print(f"    !! WARN: {len(dups)} target(s) picked by >1 query "
                  f"(leak NOT clean): {dups[:8]}{' ...' if len(dups) > 8 else ''}")

    assert len(rows) == len(shard), f"wrote {len(rows)} rows, expected {len(shard)}"
    dest = out_path if n == 1 else _shard_path(out_path, i, n)
    dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=SUBMISSION_COLS).to_csv(dest, index=False)
    print(f"\nWrote {len(rows)} rows to {dest}  (total {time.time() - t0:.1f}s)")
    if n == 1:
        assert len(rows) == n_expected, f"total {len(rows)} != expected {n_expected}"
    else:
        print(f"Shard {i}/{n} done. Merge with: --merge-shards {n} --out {args.out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

file (e.g. --datasets 2 --splits val) lets us read that pool's real MRR in
isolation.

  affine_argmin  -- affine_ranker.AffineArgminRanker: header-only, ranks by
                    ascending affine Frobenius distance. The confirmed ds3

            if loaded is not None:
                sets.append((ds, split, loaded[0], loaded[1]))
    if not sets:
        raise SystemExit("No usable prediction sets found; nothing to write.")

    n_expected = sum(len(q) for _, _, q, _ in sets)
    expected_ids = {qid for _, _, q, _ in sets for qid in q}

    if args.merge_shards:
        _merge_shards(out_path, args.merge_shards, n_expected, expected_ids)
        return

    flat: List[Tuple[int, str, str, str, Dict[str, str], str]] = []
    set_str_targets = []
    for ds, split, queries, targets in sets:
        st = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
        set_str_targets.append(st)
        for qid in sorted(queries):
            flat.append((ds, split, qid, str(queries[qid]), st, ranker_of_ds[ds]))

    i, n = (int(x) for x in args.query_shard.split("/"))
    if not (1 <= i <= n):
        raise SystemExit(f"--query-shard {args.query_shard}: need 1 <= i <= N")
    shard = [row for idx, row in enumerate(flat) if idx % n == (i - 1)]

    per_reg = PER_REG_SEC_BY_TRANSFORM.get(args.transform, DEFAULT_PER_REG_SEC)
    reg_pairs = sum(len(st) for _, _, _, _, st, rn in shard if rn == "reg_mi")
    est_sec = reg_pairs * per_reg
    print(f"\n[cost] shard {i}/{n}: {len(shard)} queries "
          f"({sum(rn=='reg_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} reg, "
          f"{sum(rn!='reg_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} header/plain)")
    print(f"[cost] {reg_pairs} {args.transform} registrations @ ~{per_reg:.2f}s = "
          f"~{est_sec/60:.1f} min (~{est_sec/3600:.2f} h) projected wall-time "
          f"(+ header/plain MI negligible)")
    for ds, split, q, t in sets:
        rn = ranker_of_ds[ds]
        npairs = len(q) * len(t)
        tag = (f"~{npairs*per_reg/60:.1f} min reg" if rn == "reg_mi"
               else "negligible (header/plain)")
        print(f"       dataset{ds}/{split} [{rn}]: {len(q)}x{len(t)} = {npairs} pairs -> {tag}")

    if args.dry_run:
        print("\n[dry-run] stopping before any ranking.")
        return

    need = {rn for *_, rn in shard}
    rankers: Dict[str, object] = {}
    if "plain_mi" in need:
        rankers["plain_mi"] = MIRanker(grid=64)
    if "reg_mi" in need:
        rankers["reg_mi"] = RegMIRanker(grid=reg_grid, type_of_transform=args.transform)
    if "affine_argmin" in need:
        rankers["affine_argmin"] = AffineArgminRanker()

    if i == 1:
        sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d]) for d, s, q, t in sets
                       if ranker_of_ds[d] == "reg_mi"), None)
        if sanity is None:
            d, s, q, t = sets[0]
            sanity = (q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d])
        _sanity_block(rankers[sanity[3]], sanity[0], sanity[1], sanity[2])

    print("\n=== ranking ===")
    t0 = time.time()
    rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
    pool_top1: Dict[Tuple[int, str], Dict[str, str]] = {}
    pool_gallery: Dict[Tuple[int, str], int] = {}
    for ds, split, qid, qpath, st_targets, rn in shard:
        ranking = rankers[rn].rank(qid, qpath, st_targets)
        rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
        pool_top1.setdefault((ds, split), {})[qid] = ranking[0]
        pool_gallery[(ds, split)] = len(st_targets)
        if len(rows) % 10 == 0:
            print(f"  ranked {len(rows)}/{len(shard)} queries "
                  f"({time.time() - t0:.1f}s elapsed)", flush=True)

    print("\n=== TOP-1 BIJECTION SANITY (each pool: are argmin targets distinct?) ===")
    for (ds, split), top1 in pool_top1.items():
        chosen = list(top1.values())
        distinct, nq, ng = len(set(chosen)), len(chosen), pool_gallery[(ds, split)]
        bij = distinct == nq == ng
        print(f"  dataset{ds}/{split}: {distinct}/{nq} distinct top-1 targets "
              f"(gallery={ng}) -> {'clean bijection' if bij else 'NOT a bijection'}")
        if not bij and n == 1:
            from collections import Counter
            dups = [t for t, c in Counter(chosen).items() if c > 1]
            print(f"    !! WARN: {len(dups)} target(s) picked by >1 query "
                  f"(leak NOT clean): {dups[:8]}{' ...' if len(dups) > 8 else ''}")

    assert len(rows) == len(shard), f"wrote {len(rows)} rows, expected {len(shard)}"
    dest = out_path if n == 1 else _shard_path(out_path, i, n)
    dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=SUBMISSION_COLS).to_csv(dest, index=False)
    print(f"\nWrote {len(rows)} rows to {dest}  (total {time.time() - t0:.1f}s)")
    if n == 1:
        assert len(rows) == n_expected, f"total {len(rows)} != expected {n_expected}"
    else:
        print(f"Shard {i}/{n} done. Merge with: --merge-shards {n} --out {args.out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.content:
1	"""Build a Kaggle-format submission by running the MI ranker over the REAL data.
2	
3	Mirrors the six prediction sets of laurence/dinov2_baseline_modal.py
4	({dataset1,2,3} x {val,test}), but ranks with one of our training-free rankers so
5	we can submit and read the real per-dataset numbers. Kaggle scores omitted queries
6	as 0 and averages ds1/ds2/ds3 x3, so writing a single-dataset (or single-split)
7	file (e.g. --datasets 2 --splits val) lets us read that pool's real MRR in
8	isolation.
9	
10	Rankers:
11	  plain_mi       -- mi_ranker.MIRanker (fast; grid 64).
12	  reg_mi         -- reg_mi_ranker.RegMIRanker (ANTs register-then-MI; grid 96).
13	                    --transform {Affine,SyN} picks the ANTs transform.
14	  affine_argmin  -- affine_ranker.AffineArgminRanker: header-only, ranks by
15	                    ascending affine Frobenius distance. The confirmed ds3
16	                    geometry leak (perfect bijection); runs in seconds.
17	
18	--ranker sets ONE ranker for every dataset. --ranker-map overrides it PER dataset,
19	e.g. "1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:plain_mi" -- the combined submission: ds1/ds3 use plain
20	MI (already ~0.98, registration only costs a tiny -0.019), only ds2 pays for
21	registration. Registration is O(N_queries * N_targets) per pool, so the script
22	prints an upfront cost estimate and projected wall-time BEFORE doing any work.
23	
24	Sharding (registration is embarrassingly parallel over queries): --query-shard i/N
25	processes the i-th of N round-robin slices of the global query list and writes a
26	PARTIAL CSV (<out>.shardI-of-N.csv). --merge-shards N concatenates the N partials
27	into the final --out and checks full coverage. A single un-sharded run (the default,
28	N=1) writes --out directly. NOTE: ANTs already multithreads each registration across
29	all local cores, so local shards give resumability, not speedup; real fan-out needs
30	multiple machines (Modal).
31	
32	Pool isolation is the key invariant: every query is ranked ONLY against its own
33	dataset's own split gallery; we never mix pools. Each ranking is the full
34	space-separated gallery, best->worst.
35	
36	No network, no Kaggle API -- this only writes a CSV locally.
37	
38	Run from the repo root (one level above this file):
39	
40	    # combined: plain MI for ds1, reg-MI affine for ds2, header leak for ds3 (377 rows)
41	    python laurence/make_submission.py --data-root . \\
42	        --ranker-map "1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:affine_argmin" --transform Affine \\
43	        --out Alvaro/generated/regmi_full_submission.csv
44	    # or from the laurence/ folder using just:
45	    #   just regmi
46	"""
47	
48	from __future__ import annotations
49	
50	import argparse
51	import sys
52	import time
53	from pathlib import Path
54	from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
55	
56	import pandas as pd
57	
58	# Repo root must be on sys.path so that Alvaro.src.* is importable.
59	# When run as `python laurence/make_submission.py` from the repo root this is
60	# already true; the guard below handles `python make_submission.py` from inside
61	# the laurence/ folder.
62	_repo_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
63	if str(_repo_root) not in sys.path:
64	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_repo_root))
65	
66	from Alvaro.src.affine_ranker import AffineArgminRanker  # noqa: E402
67	from Alvaro.src.mi_ranker import MIRanker  # noqa: E402
68	from Alvaro.src.reg_mi_ranker import RegMIRanker, REG_GRID  # noqa: E402
69	
70	RANKER_NAMES = ("plain_mi", "reg_mi", "affine_argmin")
71	
72	ALL_SPLITS = ("val", "test")
73	SUBMISSION_COLS = ["query_id", "target_id_ranking"]
74	PER_REG_SEC_BY_TRANSFORM = {"Affine": 0.49, "SyN": 1.24}
75	DEFAULT_PER_REG_SEC = 0.49
76	
77	
78	def resolve(data_root: Path, rel: str) -> Path:
79	    """Absolute image path with the .nii.gz -> .nii fallback."""
80	    p = Path(rel)
81	    p = p if p.is_absolute() else data_root / p
82	    if not p.exists() and p.suffix == ".gz":
83	        nii = p.with_suffix("")
84	        if nii.exists():
85	            return nii
86	    return p
87	
88	
89	def _load_set(
90	    data_root: Path, ds: int, split: str
91	) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]] | None:
92	    ds_dir = data_root / f"dataset{ds}"
93	    qcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_queries.csv"
94	    gcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_gallery.csv"
95	    if not qcsv.exists() or not gcsv.exists():
96	        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing CSV ({qcsv.name}/{gcsv.name}); skipping set")
97	        return None
98	
99	    def collect(csv_path: Path, id_col: str, img_col: str) -> Dict[str, Path]:
100	        present: Dict[str, Path] = {}
101	        for _, row in pd.read_csv(csv_path).iterrows():
102	            iid = str(row[id_col])
103	            p = resolve(data_root, str(row[img_col]))
104	            if p.exists():
105	                present[iid] = p
106	            else:
107	                print(f"    WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing image for {iid}: {p}")
108	        return present
109	
110	    queries = collect(qcsv, "query_id", "query_image")
111	    targets = collect(gcsv, "target_id", "target_image")
112	    if not queries or not targets:
113	        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: no usable queries/targets on disk; skipping set")
114	        return None
115	    print(f"  dataset{ds}/{split}: {len(queries)} queries, {len(targets)} targets")
116	    return queries, targets
117	
118	
119	def _sanity_block(ranker, queries: Dict[str, Path], targets: Dict[str, Path],
120	                  label: str) -> None:
121	    print(f"\n=== SANITY ({label}) — does the score separate top from tail? ===")
122	    str_targets = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
123	    for qid in list(queries)[:2]:
124	        scored = ranker.score_targets(str(queries[qid]), str_targets)
125	        top3 = scored[:3]
126	        worst_tid, worst_score = scored[-1]
127	        spread = top3[0][1] - worst_score
128	        print(f"  query {qid}:")
129	        for rank, (tid, sc) in enumerate(top3, 1):
130	            print(f"    #{rank}: {tid}  NMI={sc:.4f}")
131	        print(f"    worst: {worst_tid}  NMI={worst_score:.4f}   (top-tail spread={spread:.4f})")
132	        if spread < 0.02:
133	            print("    !! DEGENERATE: top barely above tail — MI is not discriminating here.")
134	
135	
136	def _abs_out(out: str) -> Path:
137	    """Anchor a relative --out at the repo root."""
138	    p = Path(out)
139	    return p if p.is_absolute() else _repo_root / p
140	
141	
142	def _shard_path(out_path: Path, i: int, n: int) -> Path:
143	    return out_path.with_name(f"{out_path.stem}.shard{i}-of-{n}{out_path.suffix}")
144	
145	
146	def _parse_ranker_map(spec: str | None, default_name: str, ds_ids: List[int]) -> Dict[int, str]:
147	    mapping = {ds: default_name for ds in ds_ids}
148	    if spec:
149	        for item in spec.split(","):
150	            if not item.strip():
151	                continue
152	            ds_str, name = item.split(":")
153	            name = name.strip()
154	            if name not in RANKER_NAMES:
155	                raise SystemExit(f"--ranker-map: unknown ranker '{name}'")
156	            mapping[int(ds_str)] = name
157	    return mapping
158	
159	
160	def _merge_shards(out_path: Path, n: int, n_expected: int, expected_ids: set) -> None:
161	    frames = []
162	    for i in range(1, n + 1):
163	        sp = _shard_path(out_path, i, n)
164	        if not sp.exists():
165	            raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: missing shard partial {sp}")
166	        frames.append(pd.read_csv(sp))
167	    merged = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
168	    ids = set(merged["query_id"].astype(str))
169	    dups = len(merged) - merged["query_id"].nunique()
170	    if dups:
171	        raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: {dups} duplicate query rows across shards")
172	    if ids != expected_ids:
173	        missing, extra = expected_ids - ids, ids - expected_ids
174	        raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: coverage mismatch "
175	                         f"(missing {len(missing)}, extra {len(extra)})")
176	    assert len(merged) == n_expected, f"merged {len(merged)} rows, expected {n_expected}"
177	    merged[SUBMISSION_COLS].to_csv(out_path, index=False)
178	    print(f"Merged {n} shards -> {len(merged)} rows at {out_path}")
179	
180	
181	def main(argv: List[str] | None = None) -> None:
182	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
183	    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True,
184	                    help="root that the CSV image paths are relative to")
185	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="Alvaro/generated/mi_submission.csv")
186	    ap.add_argument("--datasets", default="1,2,3",
187	                    help="comma-separated dataset ids to include (e.g. '2')")
188	    ap.add_argument("--splits", default="val,test",
189	                    help="comma-separated splits to include (default 'val,test')")
190	    ap.add_argument("--ranker", choices=list(RANKER_NAMES), default="plain_mi",
191	                    help="ranker for ALL datasets unless overridden by --ranker-map")
192	    ap.add_argument("--ranker-map", default=None,
193	                    help="per-dataset override, e.g. '1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:plain_mi'")
194	    ap.add_argument("--transform", default="Affine",
195	                    help="ANTs type_of_transform for reg_mi (Affine, SyN, ...)")
196	    ap.add_argument("--downsample", type=int, default=None,
197	                    help="reg_mi grid override (default 96); plain_mi is always 64")
198	    ap.add_argument("--query-shard", default="1/1",
199	                    help="process the i-th of N round-robin query slices (e.g. 2/4)")
200	    ap.add_argument("--merge-shards", type=int, default=0,
201	                    help="merge N shard partials into --out and exit")
202	    ap.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true",
203	                    help="print the cost estimate and exit before any ranking")
204	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
205	
206	    data_root = Path(args.data_root).resolve()
207	    ds_ids = sorted(int(x) for x in args.datasets.split(",") if x.strip())
208	    splits = [s for s in ALL_SPLITS if s in
209	              {x.strip() for x in args.splits.split(",")}]
210	    ranker_of_ds = _parse_ranker_map(args.ranker_map, args.ranker, ds_ids)
211	    reg_grid = args.downsample if args.downsample is not None else REG_GRID
212	    out_path = _abs_out(args.out)
213	    print(f"data-root={data_root}  datasets={ds_ids}  splits={splits}\n"
214	          f"ranker_map={ranker_of_ds}  transform={args.transform}  "
215	          f"plain_grid=64 reg_grid={reg_grid}")
216	
217	    sets: List[Tuple[int, str, Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]]] = []
218	    for ds in ds_ids:
219	        for split in splits:
220	            loaded = _load_set(data_root, ds, split)
221	            if loaded is not None:
222	                sets.append((ds, split, loaded[0], loaded[1]))
223	    if not sets:
224	        raise SystemExit("No usable prediction sets found; nothing to write.")
225	
226	    n_expected = sum(len(q) for _, _, q, _ in sets)
227	    expected_ids = {qid for _, _, q, _ in sets for qid in q}
228	
229	    if args.merge_shards:
230	        _merge_shards(out_path, args.merge_shards, n_expected, expected_ids)
231	        return
